title: Motorola Razr

infobox.value: **Motorola Razr V3:** 2004; 22 years ago (2004) **Motorola Razr2:** 2007; 19 years ago (2007) **Motorola Droid Razr:** 2011; 15 years ago (2011)  **Motorola Razr:** 2020; 6 years ago (2020)
[Motorola KRZR](/wiki/Motorola_KRZR "Motorola KRZR"), [Motorola PEBL](/wiki/Motorola_PEBL "Motorola PEBL"), [Motorola Moto](/wiki/Motorola_Moto "Motorola Moto")
2004; 22 years ago (2004) **Motorola Razr2:** 2007; 19 years ago (2007) **Motorola Droid Razr:** 2011; 15 years ago (2011)  **Motorola Razr:** 2020; 6 years ago (2020) |

(pronounced [/ˈreɪzər/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") like "razor"), currently stylized *motorola razr*, is a brand of [mobile phones](/wiki/Mobile_phone "Mobile phone") manufactured by [Motorola Mobility](/wiki/Motorola_Mobility "Motorola Mobility") (previously [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola "Motorola"), now [a division](/wiki/Motorola#After_2000 "Motorola") of [Lenovo](/wiki/Lenovo "Lenovo")), currently consisting of [foldable clamshell smartphones](/wiki/Foldable_smartphone "Foldable smartphone"). The Razr name was introduced with the [V3](/wiki/Motorola_RAZR_V3 "Motorola RAZR V3") model (then styled **RAZR**, also **MOTORAZR**) in 2004, which became extremely popular. The line was succeeded by the [Motorola Razr2](/wiki/Motorola_Razr2 "Motorola Razr2") flip phones in 2007. The Razr later evolved into a line of non-clamshell devices in 2011, [Droid Razr](/wiki/Droid_Razr "Droid Razr") (known simply as the "Motorola RAZR" on non-Verizon networks), bearing design similarities to the originals. Razr was revived again in 2019, this time evolving into a line of all-screen clamshell foldables that continues to this day.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Series of mobile phones by Motorola Mobility

"Razr" redirects here; not to be confused with [Razer Inc.](/wiki/Razer_Inc. "Razer Inc.").

Motorola Razr

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Developer | [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola "Motorola") (2004–2011), [Motorola Mobility](/wiki/Motorola_Mobility "Motorola Mobility") (2011–present) |

| Released | **Motorola Razr V3:** 2004; 22 years ago (2004) **Motorola Razr2:** 2007; 19 years ago (2007) **Motorola Droid Razr:** 2011; 15 years ago (2011)  **Motorola Razr:** 2020; 6 years ago (2020) |
| Lifespan | 2004–2015, 2019–present |

| Related | [Motorola KRZR](/wiki/Motorola_KRZR "Motorola KRZR"), [Motorola PEBL](/wiki/Motorola_PEBL "Motorola PEBL"), [Motorola Moto](/wiki/Motorola_Moto "Motorola Moto") |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/16/Motorola_RAZR_V3i_01.JPG/250px-Motorola_RAZR_V3i_01.JPG)](/wiki/File:Motorola_RAZR_V3i_01.JPG)

The Razr V3i was sold during the height of the popularity of the Razr series.

The **Motorola Razr** (pronounced [/ˈreɪzər/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") like "razor"), currently stylized *motorola razr*, is a brand of [mobile phones](/wiki/Mobile_phone "Mobile phone") manufactured by [Motorola Mobility](/wiki/Motorola_Mobility "Motorola Mobility") (previously [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola "Motorola"), now [a division](/wiki/Motorola#After_2000 "Motorola") of [Lenovo](/wiki/Lenovo "Lenovo")), currently consisting of [foldable clamshell smartphones](/wiki/Foldable_smartphone "Foldable smartphone"). The Razr name was introduced with the [V3](/wiki/Motorola_RAZR_V3 "Motorola RAZR V3") model (then styled **RAZR**, also **MOTORAZR**) in 2004, which became extremely popular. The line was succeeded by the [Motorola Razr2](/wiki/Motorola_Razr2 "Motorola Razr2") flip phones in 2007. The Razr later evolved into a line of non-clamshell devices in 2011, [Droid Razr](/wiki/Droid_Razr "Droid Razr") (known simply as the "Motorola RAZR" on non-Verizon networks), bearing design similarities to the originals. Razr was revived again in 2019, this time evolving into a line of all-screen clamshell foldables that continues to this day.

## Original Razr line

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Original Razr line")]

Main article: [Motorola Razr V3](/wiki/Motorola_Razr_V3 "Motorola Razr V3")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/41/MOTORAZR.png)](/wiki/File:MOTORAZR.png)

MOTORAZR logo used for the first RAZR series

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ee/Motorola_razr_v3xx_flip_phone_at%26t_wireless_taken_2023.jpg/250px-Motorola_razr_v3xx_flip_phone_at%26t_wireless_taken_2023.jpg)](/wiki/File:Motorola_razr_v3xx_flip_phone_at%26t_wireless_taken_2023.jpg)

Two RAZR V3xx phones (2006)

The [Razr V3](/wiki/Razr_V3 "Razr V3") was introduced in 2004 and became wildly popular. Motorola released several upgraded versions, including the Razr V3x in 2005 (which featured [3G](/wiki/3G "3G") connectivity), the Razr V3i, and the Razr v3xx in 2006, along with various variants. Motorola also released the [Motorola RAZR maxx V6](/wiki/Motorola_RAZR_maxx "Motorola RAZR maxx").

## Razr2 – second-generation Razr flip phones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Razr2 – second-generation Razr flip phones")]

Main article: [Motorola Razr2](/wiki/Motorola_Razr2 "Motorola Razr2")

The Razr2 was the successor to the Razr series. The Razr2 was 2 mm thinner than its predecessor but slightly wider. Some versions featured Motorola's [MotoMagx](/wiki/MotoMagx "MotoMagx") operational platform, based on the [MontaVista](/wiki/MontaVista "MontaVista") [Linux](/wiki/Linux "Linux") OS. The Razr2 was available on every US carrier, and EVDO, GSM, and HSDPA versions were released by late 2007. The Razr2 line consisted of 4 models: V8, V9, V9m, and V9x.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The phone improved picture quality, speed, and multimedia capabilities over the original Razr. It also featured an external screen with touch-sensitive buttons, which allowed users to use some of the phone's features without opening it, and Motorola's CrystalTalk technology to improve call quality and help reduce background noise.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Different color variants were released, including a Luxury Edition and a [Ferrari](/wiki/Ferrari "Ferrari") Edition.

However, Razr2 sales were not as strong as those of the original V3 series, as consumers moved to competing products.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Because Motorola relied so long upon the Razr and its derivatives[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) and was slow to develop new products in the growing market for feature-rich touchscreen and 3G phones,[[6]](#cite_note-6) the Razr appeal declined, leading Motorola to drop behind [Samsung](/wiki/Samsung_Electronics "Samsung Electronics") eventually and [LG](/wiki/LG "LG") in market share for mobile phones.[[7]](#cite_note-bloomberg2008.03.26-7) Motorola's strategy of grabbing market share by selling tens of millions of low-cost Razrs cut into margins and resulted in heavy losses in the cellular division.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) The cell phone division became part of Motorola Mobility in 2011 as a result of the Motorola split.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Droid Razr – candybar smartphones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Droid Razr – candybar smartphones")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6b/Motorola_RAZR_XT910.jpg/250px-Motorola_RAZR_XT910.jpg)](/wiki/File:Motorola_RAZR_XT910.jpg)

Motorola RAZR (XT910)

The Razr brand returned in 2011 with the introduction of the Motorola [Droid Razr](/wiki/Droid_Razr "Droid Razr") smartphone (the "Droid" name only used by [Verizon](/wiki/Verizon "Verizon") in the USA). The line shared its trademark thinness and stylized tapered corners with the original.[[11]](#cite_note-mashable-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) The line included:

* [Droid Razr](/wiki/Droid_Razr "Droid Razr"), flagship successor of the [Droid Bionic](/wiki/Droid_Bionic "Droid Bionic")
  + Droid Razr Maxx, variant with a larger battery
* [Motorola Razr V](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr_V&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Razr V (page does not exist)"), a cheaper Droid Razr released in Canada
* [Droid Razr HD](/wiki/Droid_Razr_HD "Droid Razr HD"), new flagship introduced in 2012
  + Droid Razr Maxx HD, variant with a larger battery
* [Droid Razr M](/wiki/Droid_Razr_M "Droid Razr M"), same internals as Droid Razr HD but in a smaller body
  + [Motorola RAZR i](/wiki/Motorola_RAZR_i "Motorola RAZR i"), variant with an [Intel Atom](/wiki/Intel_Atom "Intel Atom")-powered chip and no 4G
* [Motorola Razr D1](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr_D1&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Razr D1 (page does not exist)") and [D3](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr_D3&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Razr D3 (page does not exist)"), entry-level products released in Brazil in 2013

The Droid Razr HD and Droid Razr M were succeeded by the [Droid MAXX](/wiki/Droid_MAXX "Droid MAXX") and [Droid Mini](/wiki/Droid_Mini "Droid Mini"), respectively.

## Razr – foldable smartphones (since 2019)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Razr – foldable smartphones (since 2019)")]

In November 2019, Motorola Mobility revived the Razr as a [foldable smartphone](/wiki/Foldable_smartphone "Foldable smartphone") (flip phone), styled after the clamshell form factor of the original models.[[13]](#cite_note-Gartenberg-13)[[14]](#cite_note-engadget.com-14) The [Motorola Razr (2020)](/wiki/Motorola_Razr_(2020) "Motorola Razr (2020)"), also called the Razr 2019, depending on the source,[[15]](#cite_note-gsmarena-15) has a design reminiscent of the classic Razr V3. It was designed with [nostalgia](/wiki/Nostalgia "Nostalgia") in mind – to remind people of the early 21st century, a time many look back on with fondness and sentiment. It was supposed to be a capable, modern [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)")-running smartphone with a "high-tech" folding display and advanced features such as a fingerprint sensor, inside a body that already felt familiar to many. It features a vertically folding display, like the familiar V3 and other Razr models from the 2000s.

Motorola Mobility presented the phone on November 14, 2019.[[15]](#cite_note-gsmarena-15) It was priced at $1,499 and initially only available on Verizon Wireless.[[13]](#cite_note-Gartenberg-13)[[14]](#cite_note-engadget.com-14) The phone was initially expected to launch in January 2020, but was subsequently delayed until February 6, 2020.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) When it launched, it was received with mixed reviews. Many reviewers felt it was too expensive for its specifications. Its camera, battery life, weak build quality, and price were criticized, while the design and software were praised. [PC Magazine](/wiki/PC_Magazine "PC Magazine") summed it up: "Motorola [Mobility]'s gorgeous folding Razr doesn't deliver the performance you expect from a $1,500 phone".[[18]](#cite_note-18) [The Verge](/wiki/The_Verge "The Verge") wrote harshly: "I wish I could tell you exactly where I think the Motorola Razr went wrong, but there are too many options to choose from".[[19]](#cite_note-19)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/51/Motorola_Razr_40.jpg/250px-Motorola_Razr_40.jpg)](/wiki/File:Motorola_Razr_40.jpg)

Motorola Razr 40 / Razr (2023)

On September 9, 2020, Motorola Mobility announced the second generation of Motorola Razr (2020), called the Razr 5G.[[20]](#cite_note-20) The second generation included many improvements over the first generation and was initially priced at $1399.99 in the US. It was, however, also criticized for reasons similar to the first phone. The Verge wrote: "It’s better than the original in every way but still costs too much".[[21]](#cite_note-21) More successors have been released: this line consists of:

* [Motorola Razr (2020)](/wiki/Motorola_Razr_(2020) "Motorola Razr (2020)"), ann. November 2019
* Motorola Razr 5G, ann. September 2020[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* [Motorola Razr (2022)](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr_(2022)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Razr (2022) (page does not exist)"), ann. August 2022[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* [Motorola Razr 40](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr_40&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Razr 40 (page does not exist)") (known as Motorola Razr in the U.S.), ann. June 2023[[24]](#cite_note-Phelan-24)
* Motorola Razr 40 Ultra (known as Motorola Razr+ in the U.S.), ann. June 2023
* [Motorola Razr 50](/wiki/Motorola_Razr_50 "Motorola Razr 50") (known as Motorola Razr 2024 in the U.S.), ann. June 2024
* Motorola Razr 50 Ultra (known as Motorola Razr+ 2024 in the U.S.), ann. June 2024

* [Motorola Razr 60](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Razr_60&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Razr 60 (page does not exist)") (known as Motorola Razr 2025 in the U.S.), ann. April 2025

* Motorola Razr 60 Plus (known as Motorola Razr Plus 2025 in the U.S.), ann. April 2025
* Motorola Razr 60 Ultra (known as Motorola Razr Ultra 2025 in the U.S.), ann. April 2025
